course_description:
FREN1010 is the first course in ﾠthe series FREN1010, FREN1020, FREN2010 and FREN2020. It provides an introduction to basic communication skills in the French language and is designed for students with no previous experience in French, enabling beginners to reach A1 level on the Common European Frame of Reference language proficiency scale. [ Click to self-assess your CEFR level ]. Students are expected to attend FREN1010 starting with the first course meeting in Week 1.ﾠ

The in-person or external course offering may be cancelled unless a minimum of 20 students enrol.

FREN1010 is the first course inﾠthe series FREN1010, FREN1020, FREN2010 and FREN2020. It is designed to enable beginners to reach A1 level on the Common European Frame of Reference language proficiency scale. [ Click to self-assess your CEFR level ].

Students are expected to attend FREN1010 starting with the first course meeting in Week 1.ﾠ

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Public holidays:
Alternative arrangements for affected students will be announced through the Blackboard site.
Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice.
Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable.
Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full name,
Student ID, and
the Course Code
Additional information and support can be found
here
.
course_aims: The general aim of the course is to develop a basic ability to interact with people in French and to build cultural awareness. Special emphasis is placed on speaking and understanding spoken French. The course aims to achieve the CEFR A1 (http://rm.coe.int/CoERMPublicCommonSearchServices/DisplayDCTMContent?documentId=090000168045bb52) abilities.

learning_outcomes.description: acquire French vocabulary and grammar appropriate to talking about yourself, your family and interests
interact with native and non-native speakers in basic French about your identity, daily life, interests and preferences
write and read sentences and simple texts about yourself, your family, your preferences, daily life
grow cultural awareness and openness to others in a global world
acquire strategies appropriate to learning a language

assessment_details.title: Course Placement Questionnaire
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Duration: 5-10 minutes, depending on the student's background in French (no prior knowledge of French required). It is necessary to be placed in the course prior to the end of Week 2 so that students can seek alternative courses if necessary. All students are required to demonstrate they have been correctly placed in FREN1010. Failure to do so will result in a maximum grade of 3 in the course. This is a hurdle requirement. No assessment items will be marked until this has been completed. Visit https://courseplacements.languages-cultures.uq.edu.au/ Completion of this Language Placement Questionnaire is a compliance hurdle requirement in many courses. This requirement aims to ensure that you are placed in the most appropriate language course based on your current level of linguistic proficiency. Your responses, along with the possible submission of short writing or speaking sample, help us match you to a course that aligns with your previous (formal or informal) language learning experiences. While this is an unsupervised, individual online task, it is not in your best interest to use generative AI tools. If the work you submit does not reflect your actual language ability, you may be placed in a course that is not appropriate for your level, which could negatively affect your learning and performance. This may also be considered an act of student misconduct under the UQ Student Code of Conduct due to the misrepresentation of your capabilities. Please note that this course includes a hurdle assessment task where the use of generative AI or MT tools is not allowed and you will be expected to demonstrate a clear understanding of your own work without the assistance of AI or MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Navigate to the SLC Language Placement Portal
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Maximum 7 calendar days extension allowed. This is a 5 to 10 minute activity that requires no prior knowledge of French. It is necessary to be placed in the course prior to the end of Week 2 so that students can seek alternative courses if necessary.

assessment_details.title: Listening-Speaking Activity
assessment_details.category: Role play/ Simulation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessment_details.due_date: 5/09/2025 5:00 pm The activity will be open from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this activity, using the Inspera platform, you will listen to and answer questions in French. The interaction will be based on grammar and vocabulary from Units 1 and 2 as well as in-class materials. REQUIREMENTS: You are required to have stable access to internet, a working microphone and headset to complete this activity. You are highly encouraged to complete the Practice Test in Week 5 to test your technology. Technological problems are not a valid excuse for an extension. Duration: 45 minutes This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at a low A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Interaction and Listening. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This will be an online, timed task, completed in Inspera. You are allowed to use your book, dictionaries and typeit.org but no automatic translation software or other online resources. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. ** This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
online through Inspera.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Max. 28 calendar days extension.

assessment_details.title: Writing Activity
assessment_details.due_date: 26/09/2025 5:00 pm This task will be open from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In this task you will be required to demonstrate competence in writing using the grammatical structures and verb forms studied in weeks 1-8. You will write 120-180 words in response to a prompt. You will be penalised proportionately for not respecting the word limit. Duration: 25 minutes This will be an online, timed, writing task completed in Inspera. You are allowed to use your book, dictionaries and typeit.org but no automatic translation software or other online resources. You must have access to stable internet to complete this activity. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. ** This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Writing. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Listening Activity
assessment_details.due_date: 17/10/2025 5:00 pm This activity will be available from 8 am to 5 pm on Inspera.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The listening activity will be based on Défi 1 Units 1-4 (Livre + Cahier) and all materials distributed in class or on Blackboard. Students will watch a video recording of a French speaker and answer questions about the content of the interview. Duration: 25 minutes + 5 minutes perusal time. ** If you require alternative exam adjustments, please apply for them to be considered for this assessment. ** This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. assessment_details.title: Oral Interview
assessment_details.due_date: 10/11/2025 - 14/11/2025 Exam Period Week 1, date and time determined by sign up. The student's assigned timeslot will be considered the due date and time for this assessment.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The subject of the Oral Interview will be published in week 11. In preparation, students will work in groups of two to practise topics, vocabulary and structures covered in Défi1 Units 1-4. Duration: approx 4 mns per student (groups of two students are scheduled for 10 minutes) On the day of the assessment, students will participate in a simulation run by a staff member in French. They will ask and answer questions using expressions and structures studied in the course. Students are responsible for forming a group (selecting a partner and obtaining contact details) at least two weeks prior to the assessment. The preparation work is to be completed outside of class. Students are individually marked but demonstrate ability to interact in French by working with a group. If a team member requests an extension for whatever reason, the extension will only be valid for the team member and not the whole group. An official UQ ID card is required for attendance at the final oral interview. This assessment item allows you to demonstrate competency at A1 level on the CEFR language proficiency scale for Spoken Production, Spoken Interaction and Listening. ( Link to Common European Frame of Reference ) This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This Oral assessment will be recorded.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
This is an end of semester assessment during exam period. Approved extensions will be rescheduled based on assessors' availability.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.
learning_resources.other_course_materials: If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Défi 1/A1 Hybrid Livre élève & eBook code (French only version) Textbook by Fatiha Chahi, Monique Denyer, Audrey Gloanec. Editions Maison des Langues, France, 2018. We recommend purchasing through InText: https://www.languageint.com.au/ Recommended Item Description Further Requirement Collins-Robert: Dictionnaire Francais-Anglais/Anglais-Français Dictionary (online or in print)

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Grammar practice Speaking and writing tasks in class and for homework. Learning outcomes: L01, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Communicative tasks Pairwork & small group speaking tasks (focus on communication, accuracy, pronunciation). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Online Listening tasks Listening for specific info (focus on comprehension and/or pronunciation); discussion of cultural conventions. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Reading tasks Reading for specific info (focus on comprehension, vocabulary, structure, cultural information). Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Exploration of language learning strategies Learning outcomes: L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Writing tasks Communicate in written French (focus on grammatical structures & vocabulary). Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Cultural understanding Relating texts and language issues (eg. "tu"/"vous") to cultural practices. Learning outcomes: L04

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
